channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: AustralianSuper reshuffles portfolio, reduces stake in CSL and James Hardie
item.description: AustralianSuper, Australia's largest superannuation fund, has significantly reduced its holdings in key Australian stocks, including CSL and James Hardie, as part of a strategic shift in its investmen...

item.title: Wells Fargo cuts energy sector rating amid limited long-term oil price outlook
item.description: The Wells Fargo Investment Institute (WFII) has downgraded its rating for the S&P 500 energy sector from 'neutral' to 'unfavourable,' citing limited prospects for a sustained oil price premium despite...

item.title: Rajya Sabha Chairman Rejects Impeachment Motion Against Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar
item.description: The Rajya Sabha Chairman CP Radhakrishnan has rejected the impeachment motion moved against Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar, ending a major opposition effort to seek his removal. The motion ...

item.title: Proposed ceasefire and peace plan sent to US and Iran amid Hormuz tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran have both received a proposal aiming to stop ongoing hostilities and open a path toward long-term peace. This development comes shortly after President Donald Trump threaten...

item.title: Israeli Prime Minister Replaces Chief of Staff Over Racist Comments and Internal Party Trouble
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu don remove him acting chief of staff, Ziv Agmon, after some leaked audio come out wey show say the man make racist comments. According to Netanyahu, Agmon go ...

item.title: Construction of Border Wall on Mount Cristo Rey Begins, Sparking Local Controversy
item.description: Blasting activities have begun on the south side of Mount Cristo Rey, a cherished landmark on the border of New Mexico and Texas, to make way for the construction of a border wall. This move follows a...

item.title: US Supreme Court paves the way for dismissal of Steve Bannon's contempt conviction
item.description: The US Supreme Court has ruled that the Justice Department can proceed with dismissing the contempt of Congress case against Steve Bannon, an ally of former President Donald Trump. Bannon was convicte...

item.title: Masked Men Breach Delhi Assembly Security and Place Bouquet in Speaker's Car
item.description: On April 6, 2026, Delhi police detained three suspects after a security breach at the Delhi Assembly. The incident took place when a vehicle registered in Uttar Pradesh broke through Gate No. 2 of the...

item.title: Savannah Guthrie Returns to 'Today' Amid Ongoing Search for Her Missing Mother
item.description: US television anchor Savannah Guthrie made her emotional return to NBC's Today show on Monday, more than two months after her mother, Nancy Guthrie, went missing from her Arizona home. Opening the sho...

item.title: Australian Tourists Adjust Travel Plans Despite Middle East Conflict
item.description: Despite the ongoing conflict in Iran, Australian tourists continue to head to Europe, adapting their travel plans to avoid cancellations and minimise losses. Travel agents across the continent report ...

item.title: China shifts focus to support Africa's industrialisation and value addition in global trade
item.description: As Tanzania continues to push for industrialisation and increased exports, China is changing its approach to engaging with Africa. During the 14th Ministerial Conference of the World Trade Organizatio...

item.title: Trump discusses Iran military operation during White House Easter Egg Roll
item.description: During the White House Easter Egg Roll, President Donald Trump addressed the attending families and children, diverging from the planned festivities to discuss recent military operations involving the...

item.title: JPMorgan CEO urges US to strengthen policies to maintain economic and military leadership
item.description: Jamie Dimon, the CEO of JPMorgan Chase & Co, emphasised the need for the United States to strengthen its economic and military capabilities to preserve its global leadership. In a letter to shareholde...

item.title: House Official Clarifies No Intention to Disrespect Ombudsman
item.description: A spokesperson for the House of Representatives has issued a statement clarifying that no disrespect was intended towards the Office of the Ombudsman. The clarification follows concerns raised regardi...

item.title: Lee Andrews faces scrutiny over exaggerated claims about career and personal life
item.description: Lee Andrews, the husband of Katie Price, has come under public scrutiny following revelations from his ex-fiancée, Alana Percival, regarding a series of alleged false claims. Since marrying Katie Pric...

item.title: Government to Host Public Consultations on Vaal Special Economic Zone Development
item.description: The Department of Trade, Industry and Competition (dtic), alongside the Gauteng Provincial Government and local municipalities, will be holding public consultations on the planned Vaal Special Economi...

item.title: Rukia Nusra Excelling in U.S. College Athletics Through Dedication and Talent
item.description: Rukia Nusra, a young Kenyan athlete studying in the United States, has gained attention for her incredible performances in college athletics, where she continues to set new records and inspire many. O...

item.title: Four fatalities in two separate taxi crashes in Mpumalanga during Easter weekend
item.description: Four people tragically lost their lives in two separate taxi accidents in Mpumalanga on Monday, marking a grim end to the Easter long weekend. The first incident occurred on the N2 between eMkhondo an...

item.title: Tanzanian Clergy Call for Moral Renewal and National Unity in Easter Messages
item.description: During the 2026 Easter celebrations, religious leaders across Tanzania raised serious concern about what they described as a decline in moral values and growing social divisions. From Dar es Salaam to...

item.title: Seven Foreign Nationals Remanded in UAPA Terror Investigation
item.description: In Delhi, a special NIA court has remanded seven foreign nationals—six Ukrainians and one US citizen—to judicial custody for 30 days in connection with a terror case registered under the Unlawful Acti...
